cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is asking what Brad is trying to make Radha believe by sharing his own experience. The options are A through D. First, let me read through Brad's response again. He starts by saying it's a great question but difficult to answer. He mentions that his own sense of humor developed over time. He was not funny when younger but now can make people laugh. He also notes that sometimes what he finds funny others don't, and that improving wit is an ongoing process. He talks about different forms of humor and individual styles. He emphasizes the importance of language fluency, playing with words, seeing irony, and linking unrelated things. He also mentions laughing frequently, appreciating others' jokes, finding humor in daily situations, observing surroundings, practicing in his head, watching comedies, and having confidence in one's style. He adds a caution about using humor in business settings. Now, the key part is that he's sharing his personal experience to convey something to Radha. He starts by saying he didn't have a good sense of humor when younger but developed it over time. The main point here is that humor isn't fixed; it can be developed. Looking at the options, (A) says a sense of humor can be developed. That aligns with Brad's experience of developing his humor over time. Option B is about impressing others, which he does mention as a benefit, but the main focus of his personal story is about how he developed it.
